name: icu_beds
description: an ICU bed-allocation world across wards with bed counts; actions admit, transfer, discharge as beds free up

card.description: an ICU bed-allocation world across wards with bed counts; actions admit, transfer, discharge as beds free up

rules: Each ward has a fixed bed capacity; occupancy of a ward never exceeds its capacity.

admit: seat a waiting (or perceived 'pending') patient into a ward that has a free bed; with no params, take the head of the waitlist and the lowest-named ward with space. If no bed is available the patient keeps waiting.

transfer: relocate an admitted patient to a different ward that has a free bed; the source frees a bed and the target fills one, so total occupancy is unchanged. With no params, move the lowest-acuity patient from the fullest ward to the ward with the most free space.
discharge: release an admitted patient, freeing a bed in their ward; with no params, discharge the lowest-acuity patient.
Beds free up via discharge and transfer-out, which makes waiting patients admissible on a later admit.
Derived views (occupancy, total_capacity, occupied_beds, free_beds, waiting_count) are recomputed every step; time advances by one.

emit.report: ICU status @ t{time}: {occupied_beds}/{total_capacity} beds occupied, {free_beds} free, {waiting_count} waiting. Last event: {last_event}.

objectives.name: access
objectives.goal: minimize the number of patients waiting for an ICU bed

objectives.name: utilization
objectives.goal: maximize bed utilization without ever exceeding capacity

objectives.name: safety
objectives.goal: never exceed any ward's bed capacity
